{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai[[ja:神殿祭]]}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{Interlanguage link|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ", " {{Interlanguage link|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}}, the “ spring {{Interlanguage link|Kōreisai|en}} ", the " fall {{Interlanguage link|Kōreisai|en}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . [[fr:Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Festival du sanctuaire]]
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references /> {{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinden-sai]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinden-sai]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinden-sai]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinden-sai]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Shrine Festival]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinden-sai]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinden-sai]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Shrine Festival]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Shrine Festival]][[en:Shrine Festival]] [[Category:bump]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai[[ja:神殿祭]]}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{Interlanguage link|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ", " {{Interlanguage link|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}}, the “ spring {{Interlanguage link|Kōreisai|en}} ", the " fall {{Interlanguage link|Kōreisai|en}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . [[fr:Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Festival du sanctuaire]]
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references /> {{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai[[ja:神殿祭]]}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ", " {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}}, the “ spring {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} ", the " fall {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . [[fr:Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Festival du sanctuaire]]
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . == Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . == Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai[[ja:神殿祭]]}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ", " {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}}, the “ spring {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} ", the " fall {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . [[fr:Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Festival du sanctuaire]]
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references /> {{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai[[ja:神殿祭]]}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .

It also includes the “ {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ", " {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}}, the “ spring {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} ", the " fall {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .

This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . == Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai[[ja:神殿祭]]}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ", " {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}}, the “ spring {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} ", the " fall {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kōreisai|en}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . [[fr:Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Festival du sanctuaire]]
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references /> {{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] revision.text:
{{皇室祭祀}}'''神殿祭'''（しんでんさい）は、[[日本]]の伝統的な神道の儀式であり、[[春分の日]]と[[秋分の日]]にそれぞれ行われる[[祭り]]。自然から受ける恩恵への感謝を示すために執り行われ、神々への謝意を捧げる<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁 |url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html |website=www.kunaicho.go.jp |access-date=2024-04-15}}</ref>。
== 概要 == 神殿祭は、明治時代から続く日本の皇室の伝統的な神道の大祭であり、春分の日と秋分の日に宮中の神殿で行われる。明治41年（1908年）に制定された皇室祭祀例に基づき、明治時代から続く伝統である。この儀式は、旧八神殿にて天神地祇を讃えることを目的とし、日本の諸神々への敬意や感謝を表す<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-04-15 |language=ja |first=精選版 |last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及}}</ref>。
「大祭」カテゴリーに属する皇室行事であり、直接天皇が執り行う場合もあれば掌典長が主導する場合もある。また、「[[元始祭]]」や「[[紀元節|紀元節祭]]」「[[皇霊祭|春季皇霊祭]]」「[[皇霊祭|秋季皇霊祭]]」「新嘗・新嘗」なども含まれる<ref name=":1" />。
== 春季神殿祭 == 春季神殿祭は春分の日に行われる。この祭りは、新しい季節の始まりを喜び、神々へ感謝を示すために特別に設けられている<ref name=":0" />。
== 秋季神殿祭 == 秋季神殿祭も同様に秋分の日に行われる。この時は、一年間の豊かな収穫に感謝し、また、尊敬を表す<ref name=":0" />。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{デフォルトソート:しんてんさい}} [[Category:皇室の儀式]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[神殿祭]] to [[Shinden-sai]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />
{{translated page|ja|神殿祭|version=103699027|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|[[simple:user:immanuelle/Shrine Festival]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Shrine Festival]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Shrine Festival]] }}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. == Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />
{{translated page|ja|神殿祭|version=103699027|comment=Imported full ja history}}

This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神殿祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神殿祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神殿祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}{{Nihongo|'''Shrine Festival'''|神殿祭|Shinden-sai}} is a traditional Japanese Shinto [[:en:Festival|Festival]] that takes place on the days of the [[:en:Spring_equinox|spring]] and [[:en:Equinox|autumn equinoxes]]. It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
==Footnotes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <references group="note" /> <references />

It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .

It is held to show gratitude for the blessings received from nature and to offer thanks to the gods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> == Overview == Shinden-sai is a major traditional Shinto festival of the Japanese imperial family, which has taken place since the Meiji era and is celebrated at palace shrines on the days of the spring equinox and the autumn equinox . It was established in 1908 (Meiji 41) according to the Code of Imperial Rites, it is a tradition that has continued since the Meiji era. This ceremony aims to pay homage to the celestial and earthly deities in the {{ill|Hasshinden|lt=ancient shrine of the Eight Gods|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, expressing respect and gratitude to the various deities of Japan. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
This is an imperial event that falls into the Category of {{ill|Grand Festival|ja|大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Festival|qq=}} and is sometimes celebrated directly by the emperor, and sometimes led by the chief priest. It also includes the “ {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ", " {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}}, the “ spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} ", the " fall {{ill|Kōreisai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=}} " and " [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] » <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典,世界大百科事典内言及|first=精選版|title=神殿祭(しんでんさい)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E6%AE%BF%E7%A5%AD-299475|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> .
== Spring Shrine Festival == The Spring Shrine Festival takes place on the day of the spring equinox. This festival is specially reserved to celebrate the start of a new season and to express gratitude to the gods <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
== Autumn Shrine Festival == The Autumn Sanctuary Festival also takes place on the day of the [[:en:Equinox|autumnal equinox]] . At this time, we express our gratitude and respect for the year's bountiful harvest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> .
